creator: DtxdF@disroot.org
text:
(In reply to commit-hook from comment #2)

Hi Muhammad,

Thank you for reviewing and confirming my port.

> When adding new port and submitting git-formatted patch also add the port in the <CATEGORY>/Makefile for this instance sysutils/Makefile. No need to do anything on this as I am doing it for now but for next case.

As a doubt: updating `<CATEGORY>/Makefile` does not generate a problem not being a committer? For example, if I submit a port and it is in the tracker for a while without any revision, won't `<CATEGORY>/Makefile` be out of date requiring to resolve this conflict? Since a committer who wants to accept a port should have an updated index, he could easily update that file.

I have no problem in doing so, I just want to know if such a task does not generate a problem for the new ports I want to send.

Thanks!
